data.current_description: Deletes the folder with the given Id.
data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes the folder with the specified ID. This operation removes the folder and may affect any lists or items contained within it. Requires the folder ID as a path parameter. Returns a success response with no content on successful deletion.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are too minimal. Added clarity about permanence, potential side effects on contained items, required parameters, and expected response. This helps AI agents understand the destructive nature of the operation and when it's appropriate to use.

message: Operation 'put-/crm/v3/lists/{listId}/memberships/remove_remove' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Summary is clear and appropriate. Description lacks details about the request body structure (list of record IDs) and what the response contains. Added clarification about input format and response content to improve AI agent understanding of how to construct requests and interpret responses.

data.current_description:
Remove the records provided from the list. Records that do not exist or that are not members of the list are ignored.

This endpoint only works for lists that have a `processingType` of `MANUAL` or `SNAPSHOT`.
data.description_suggestion: Remove specified records from a list. Accepts a list of record IDs to remove in the request body. Records that do not exist or are not members of the list are silently ignored. This endpoint only works for lists with a `processingType` of `MANUAL` or `SNAPSHOT`. Returns a response indicating the operation status and any records that were successfully removed.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and appropriate. Description lacks details about the request body structure (list of record IDs) and what the response contains. Added clarification about input format and response content to improve AI agent understanding of how to construct requests and interpret responses.

data.current_summary: Move a List to a Folder
data.current_description: Move a list to a specified folder. The list will be relocated from its current folder to the target folder. Returns the updated list object with its new folder assignment.
data.description_suggestion: Move a list to a specified folder. Requires the list ID and target folder ID in the request. The list will be relocated from its current folder to the target folder. Returns the updated list object reflecting the new folder assignment.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate but could be slightly more polished. Original description is too vague about inputs and outputs. Added clarity about required inputs (list ID and folder ID), the relocation action, and what is returned to improve AI agent understanding of how to use this operation.

data.current_description: This moves the folder from its current location to a new location. It updates the parent of this folder to the new Id given.
data.summary_suggestion: Move folder to new parent location
data.description_suggestion: Move a folder to a new parent folder location. Updates the folder's parent reference to the specified new parent folder ID. Returns the updated folder object with its new location metadata. The folder and new parent must both exist and be valid.
data.reasoning: Summary uses passive voice and lacks action clarity. Description is repetitive and lacks information about return value and validation requirements. Improved summary uses active voice and is more specific. Enhanced description clarifies the return value and adds context about validation for AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: Restore a previously deleted list by **ILS list ID**. Deleted lists are eligible to be restored up-to 90-days after the list has been deleted.
data.description_suggestion: Restore a previously deleted list using its list ID. Deleted lists can only be restored within 90 days of deletion. Returns the restored list object with all original properties and memberships. Restoring a list makes it active again and accessible for operations.
data.reasoning: Description lacks information about what is returned and the side effects of restoration (list becomes active again). Enhanced description clarifies the return value and explains the state change that occurs, helping AI agents understand the full impact of this operation.

data.current_summary: Get lists record is member of
data.current_description: For given record provide lists this record is member of.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve lists containing a specific record
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all lists that contain the specified record. Requires the object type ID and record ID as parameters. Returns a collection of list definitions that include this record as a member. Useful for understanding a record's list memberships across the system.

data.current_description: Create a new list with the provided object list definition.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new list with the specified definition and configuration. Accepts a ListCreateRequest object containing the list name, record type, processing type, and other configuration parameters. Returns the newly created list object with its assigned list ID and metadata. The list will be empty until records are added to it.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate but description is vague about what inputs are required and what is returned. Improved version specifies the request schema details, return value structure, and initial state of the created list for better AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'get-/crm/v3/lists/folders_getAll' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, inconsistent_terminology. Summary says 'Retrieves a folder' but the operation actually retrieves a folder hierarchy with all nested folders. Description uses inconsistent terminology ('childNodes' vs 'child lists'). Improved version clarifies that this retrieves a hierarchy, explains the recursive structure, and clarifies the child list behavior for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: Retrieves a folder and recursively includes all folders via the childNodes attribute.  The child lists field will be empty in all child nodes. Only the folder retrieved will include the child lists in that folder.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve folder hierarchy with nested folders and lists
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the folder structure including all nested child folders recursively. The root folder returned includes its child lists, but child folders in the hierarchy will have empty child lists fields. Use this to understand the complete folder organization and list containment structure.
data.reasoning: Summary says 'Retrieves a folder' but the operation actually retrieves a folder hierarchy with all nested folders. Description uses inconsistent terminology ('childNodes' vs 'child lists'). Improved version clarifies that this retrieves a hierarchy, explains the recursive structure, and clarifies the child list behavior for better AI agent understanding.
